hypothesis: A Transformer encoder-decoder with 150 cm⁻¹ patch-based spectrum embedding and a jointly trained spectral reconstruction alignment network, pretrained on large simulated MD-generated IR spectra and fine-tuned on NIST gas-phase IR data, can accurately identify molecular components in binary and ternary mixture IR spectra (including experimental ATR-IR measurements), achieving higher Top-k accuracy and Tanimoto similarity than models lacking the reconstruction alignment or patch embedding.
